note: Repixelizer now has one live reconstruction engine: phase-field in src/repixelizer/phase_field.py. The old tray-based optimizer is gone.
source_structure is tracked alongside source_fidelity because lattice-only scoring could rank visibly better outputs as worse.
The d9fa411 phase-field tuning pass was a real regression on the pinned badge case; reverting it restores the known-good badge result exactly.

Audit pass clarified note boundaries: the algorithm map remains the live control-flow truth, the implementation plan remains the forward hypothesis ledger, and hosted-auth-architecture is explicitly future design rather than landed auth machinery.

Hosted auth was split into a reusable GameCult hosted-access architecture plus a thin Repixelizer-specific access profile. Recommended extraction path is an embedded shared gamecult_access layer first, with dedicated service only after a second app proves the need.

Hosted convenience is the monetization path for Repixelizer; keep the code open, let self-hosting remain the escape hatch, and do not prioritize native desktop packaging unless it clearly beats hosted access on revenue, support burden, or strategy.

Added dense landscape fixture tests/fixtures/real/dense-landscape.png plus tracked fixed-512 output. Current automatic lattice inference on the 1254px source undershoots to 251x251, so README previews now include that case with the fixed 512x512 result beside Lanczos.

Dense landscape auto inference misses high because spacing stats split between false small-cell harmonics and mushier real-cell intervals. The spacing spectrum actually guides toward 415/418 and 251, but src/repixelizer/inference.py caps automatic major-axis candidates at 256, silently discarding the denser 415/418 branch; among the surviving sizes, reranking picks 251. Current phase/coherence scoring on the fixture is much stronger around 415-418 than at 251.

Relaxed automatic candidate search so high guided sizes from the spacing spectrum can survive above the old 256 cap. On tests/fixtures/real/dense-landscape.png, auto inference now lands at 418x418 instead of 251x251; still short of the fixed 512x512 target, but no longer throwing away most of the detail before scoring starts.
